evidence: QueryExecutor interface methods share a consistent parameter order: (lemma, pattern/deprel, minLogDice, maxResults) — learnable and predictable
HTTP query parameter 'top' is used consistently for max-results across /api/concordance/examples and all /api/semantic-field/* endpoints after the api-surface-finish rename
CollocateQueryHelper.executeCollocateSearch(String bcqlPattern, String lemma, boolean withStoredHits) has pattern before lemma — reverse of the QueryExecutor convention (lemma first, pattern second)

CollocateQueryHelper.buildAndRankCollocates takes @Nullable Map<String,String> posMap as the second parameter between freqMap and headwordFreq — an optional parameter inserted between two required ones rather than placed last

SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern has two public overloads: (seed, RelationConfig, ExploreOptions) and (seed, relName, bcqlPattern, simplePattern, ExploreOptions); both are public, leaking the low-level 4-string overload as part of the public API
compareCollocateProfiles(Set<String> seedNouns, double minLogDice, int maxPerNoun): maxPerNoun is named differently from topCollocates/maxResults used elsewhere for the same concept
ExploreOptions constructor parameter order: (topCollocates, nounsPerSeed, minLogDice, minShared) — logDice before minShared is consistent with the QueryExecutor convention
handleSemanticFieldExamples uses a 'max' parameter while all other exploration handlers use 'top' for the same concept — inconsistent HTTP parameter name for the same limit concept

strengths: QueryExecutor interface has a consistent (lemma, pattern, minLogDice, maxResults) parameter order across all 5 query methods
HTTP 'top' parameter standardized for max-results across concordance and exploration endpoints
ExploreOptions bundles tuning parameters into one record — avoids multi-argument creep for callers
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: Issues concentrate on two boundaries: (a) parameter ordering inconsistencies at the package-private level (executeCollocateSearch reverses lemma/pattern, buildAndRankCollocates places an optional parameter mid-list), and (b) vocabulary drift for the max-results concept ('maxPerNoun', 'max' in HTTP vs 'top'/'topCollocates').
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The public QueryExecutor API is well-shaped with consistent conventions. Deductions come from internal API inconsistencies (CollocateQueryHelper parameter order and optional-mid-list placement) that create friction for contributors working in the query package, and the 'max' vs 'top' HTTP parameter inconsistency which affects API clients. Neither is severe but both reflect unconsolidated conventions across related surfaces.

issues.summary: CollocateQueryHelper.executeCollocateSearch puts pattern before lemma, reversing the established lemma-first convention
evidence: executeCollocateSearch(String bcqlPattern, String lemma, boolean withStoredHits) — pattern first
QueryExecutor.findCollocations(String lemma, String cqlPattern, ...) — lemma first
All QueryExecutor methods follow lemma-first convention; CollocateQueryHelper is the only deviation
issues.suggestion: Reorder to executeCollocateSearch(String lemma, String bcqlPattern, boolean withStoredHits) to match the QueryExecutor convention

issues.summary: buildAndRankCollocates places the optional @Nullable posMap parameter between two required parameters
evidence: buildAndRankCollocates(Map<String,Long> freqMap, @Nullable Map<String,String> posMap, long headwordFreq, double minLogDice, int maxResults)
posMap is nullable/optional but is positioned between freqMap and headwordFreq — standard convention is to put optional parameters last
issues.suggestion: Move posMap to the last position: buildAndRankCollocates(Map<String,Long> freqMap, long headwordFreq, double minLogDice, int maxResults, @Nullable Map<String,String> posMap)

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExamples uses 'max' HTTP parameter while all sibling handlers use 'top' for the same concept
evidence: API clients using 'top' for examples endpoint would silently get the default 10 instead of an error
issues.suggestion: Change the examples endpoint to use 'top' as the parameter name, consistent with all other endpoints that limit result count

root_causes: Parameter ordering conventions were applied to the QueryExecutor interface but not consistently to its internal helper class CollocateQueryHelper
HTTP parameter naming was standardized for 'top' in most handlers but one handler missed the memo
